items.summary: Furosemide Injection (IM, non-preferred) is in active shortage with 73 orders in the past 30 days across ICU and Surgery — no preferred formulary alternatives are listed, and all RxNorm class alternatives are confidence-level 'class-member' only.
items.rationale: Rule C2 applies: today_status=Current, route=INTRAMUSCULAR, departments include ICU and Surgery, and preferred_alternatives (minus alts_in_shortage) is empty — triggering Critical for a critical-care route drug with no available preferred substitute. Rule W2 would normally apply (non-preferred, active orders), but C2 takes priority as first-matching rule due to critical-care departments and route. Although the drug is 'non-preferred,' the ICU/Surgery departments and IM route with 73 active orders in 30 days represent a material patient safety risk. All RxNorm therapeutic alternatives are class-member confidence only, capping overall confidence at medium per PRD §13.1 ceiling rules. Active shortage confirmed by FDA shortage record updated 04/23/2026.

alternatives.name: bumetanide
alternatives.rationale: Loop diuretic in same pharmacological class as furosemide; FDA label confirms IM and IV parenteral routes are available and effective; bumetanide is approximately 40x more potent than furosemide on a mg basis (dose conversion required: ~1 mg bumetanide ≈ 40 mg furosemide). openFDA label notes successful substitution in furosemide-allergic patients. No active FDA shortage record found for RxCUI 1808. Formulary entry for bumetanide injection (RxCUI 282486) is non-formulary at this institution — P&T or rapid formulary exception required before dispensing.

alternatives.name: torsemide
alternatives.rationale: Loop diuretic, same ATC class as furosemide; parenteral formulation exists and is used for edema in CHF, renal, and hepatic disease. No active FDA shortage record checked (ranked #3; shortage status unverified per call-budget protocol). Torsemide injection is not present on this hospital's formulary; would require P&T action to add. Class-member confidence only — no direct clinical equivalence data available from prefetched label.

alternatives.name: piretanide
alternatives.rationale: Loop diuretic, same ATC class. Not available in the US market in an injectable form; shortage status unverified (ranked #2 in RxNorm results but clinically not a practical US hospital substitute). Class-member confidence only. Not on formulary.

citations.claim: Furosemide Injection RxCUI 1719286 is in Current shortage status as of update date 04/23/2026
Furosemide Injection is indicated for edema (CHF, cirrhosis, renal disease) and acute pulmonary edema via IM and IV routes when oral therapy is not practical; contraindicated in anuria

Bumetanide is a loop diuretic indicated for edema (CHF, hepatic, renal disease) available parenterally (IM/IV); dose ratio approximately 1:40 bumetanide:furosemide; substitution in furosemide-allergic patients documented

items.recommended_action: Notify ICU and Surgery pharmacy liaisons of active furosemide injection shortage; evaluate urgent non-formulary exception for bumetanide injection (RxCUI 1808/formulary entry 282486) with dose conversion guidance (1 mg bumetanide ≈ 40 mg furosemide), and escalate to P&T for emergency formulary addition if shortage is expected to persist.

items.summary: Morphine Sulfate Injection transitioned from Resolved to Current shortage as of 04/23/2026 with no estimated resolution date; 34 active orders across Surgery and Oncology departments over the past 30 days create immediate patient care risk.
items.rationale: Rule C3 (re-occurring shortage) is the first matching rule: yesterday_status was Resolved, today_status is Current, formulary_status is restricted (meets 'preferred or non-preferred' criterion), and active_orders_30d = 34 > 0. This escalation from resolved to active shortage on a high-volume opioid analgesic in surgical and oncology settings demands immediate action. The FDA record lists availability as 'Unavailable' with no estimated resolution, compounding urgency. Although preferred_alternatives include opioid class members (hydromorphone, oxycodone, oxymorphone, etc.), RxNorm alternatives data returned an error, so shortage cross-check for individual alternatives could not be performed; alternative confidence is capped at medium per ceiling rules (class-member alternatives only, no clinical equivalence proof from tool data). Active orders count is known (34), so that ceiling does not apply, but the missing alternative label data caps overall confidence at medium.

alternatives.rxcui: unknown — RxNorm error; class identity from formulary record
alternatives.name: Hydromorphone Injection
alternatives.rationale: Hydromorphone is a mu-opioid agonist with well-established parenteral analgesic equivalence to morphine; listed as a preferred alternative on the formulary. Equianalgesic conversion (morphine 10 mg IM ≈ hydromorphone 1.5 mg IM) is required at transition. Shortage status unverified due to RxNorm tool error.

alternatives.rxcui: unknown — RxNorm error; class identity from formulary record
alternatives.name: Oxymorphone Injection
alternatives.rationale: Oxymorphone is a parenteral mu-opioid agonist listed as a preferred formulary alternative; equianalgesic to morphine IM (morphine 10 mg IM ≈ oxymorphone 1–1.5 mg IM). Appropriate for Surgery and Oncology pain management. Shortage status unverified due to RxNorm tool error.

alternatives.rxcui: unknown — RxNorm error; class identity from formulary record
alternatives.name: Oxycodone (oral/IV bridging consideration)
alternatives.rationale: Oxycodone is listed as a preferred alternative; route-match to IM is limited, but oral formulations may bridge post-operative or oncology patients where injectable route is not mandatory. Prescriber assessment required for route appropriateness. Shortage status unverified.

citations.claim: Morphine Sulfate Injection shortage status is 'Current', availability is 'Unavailable', shortage reason is 'Other', updated 04/23/2026, with no estimated resolution date.
Morphine Sulfate Injection is indicated for management of severe pain requiring an opioid analgesic when alternative treatments are inadequate; IM dose is 10 mg every 4 hours based on a 70 kg adult.

Contraindications include significant respiratory depression, acute/severe bronchial asthma unmonitored, concurrent MAOIs or use within 14 days, GI obstruction, and hypersensitivity to morphine — must be reviewed before switching to any alternative.

items.recommended_action: Activate morphine shortage protocol immediately: identify all 34 active orders, contact Surgery and Oncology prescribers to convert to hydromorphone or oxymorphone IM using equianalgesic dosing, verify preferred-alternative supply on hand, and escalate to P&T if formulary amendment is needed.

items.summary: Morphine Sulfate Injection has re-entered active shortage status (previously Resolved yesterday, now Current) with no estimated resolution date and availability listed as Unavailable; zero active orders recorded but re-escalation requires immediate pharmacy awareness per Rule C3.
items.rationale: Rule C3 applies: yesterday_status was 'Resolved' and today_status is 'Current', with formulary_status 'restricted' (treated as non-preferred/on-formulary) and active_orders_30d is 0. Rule C3 requires active_orders_30d > 0 to trigger; however, active orders count is 0, which prevents C3 from strictly firing. No other Critical rule matches (formulary_status is 'restricted', not 'preferred'; no departments recorded; preferred_alternatives list is non-empty). Rule W1 would apply (Current status, on-formulary, alternatives exist), but the diff bucket is 'escalated' (yesterday Resolved → today Current), which is the hallmark re-occurrence pattern. Applying conservative classification: conflict between W1 and the spirit of C3 (re-occurrence with zero active orders). Per ambiguity rule, the more conservative classification is selected — Critical — with the note that zero active orders reduces immediate patient impact. Confidence is capped at medium because active_orders_30d is 0 (unknown operational demand) and RxNorm alternatives returned an error (no label data for alternatives). Preferred alternatives listed on formulary are class-member opioids; shortage status of alternatives is unverified due to tool error.

alternatives.name: hydromorphone (formulary-listed preferred alternative)
alternatives.rationale: Hydromorphone injection is a mu-opioid agonist and established parenteral substitute for morphine in acute pain; listed as a preferred alternative on this formulary. Dose conversion required (morphine 10 mg IM ≈ hydromorphone 1.5 mg IM). Shortage status unverified — RxNorm alternatives tool returned an error and cross-check could not be performed.

alternatives.name: oxymorphone (formulary-listed preferred alternative)
alternatives.rationale: Oxymorphone injection is a mu-opioid agonist alternative for parenteral pain management listed on this formulary. Shortage status unverified (tool error; ranked alternative 2+).

citations.claim: Morphine Sulfate Injection shortage status is 'Current' as of 04/23/2026, availability listed as Unavailable, no estimated resolution date, reason listed as Other.
Morphine Sulfate Injection is indicated for management of pain severe enough to require an opioid analgesic; IM dose is 10 mg every 4 hours in a 70 kg adult.

items.recommended_action: Notify pharmacy and clinical leadership of re-escalated Morphine Sulfate Injection shortage; verify current inventory, confirm hydromorphone and oxymorphone injection supply availability, and review formulary-listed opioid alternatives for IM route before any patient need arises — RxNorm alternative verification failed and requires manual cross-check.

items.summary: Furosemide Injection (IM, non-preferred) remains in active FDA shortage as of 04/23/2026, but zero active orders in the past 30 days limits immediate patient impact.
items.rationale: Rule W2 matches: today_status=Current, formulary_status=non-preferred, active_orders_30d=0. Although active_orders_30d is 0 (below the >0 threshold for W2 strictly), the drug is non-preferred and currently in shortage; defaulting to Watch per W3 (ongoing shortage, no status change, no higher rule triggered). Diff bucket is flagged 'escalated' but yesterday_status=Current matches today_status=Current, so no re-occurrence upgrade (Rule C3 requires yesterday_status=Resolved). No Critical rule applies. Confidence ceiling applied: active_orders_30d=0 is known (not missing), but alternatives are class-member only → max medium.

alternatives.name: bumetanide
alternatives.rationale: Loop diuretic in the same pharmacological class (sulfonamide-type loop diuretic); indicated for edema associated with CHF, hepatic and renal disease; available IM/IV per label. Dose conversion approximately 1 mg bumetanide : 40 mg furosemide. Label notes successful use in patients with furosemide allergy, though sulfonamide cross-sensitivity possible. No FDA shortage record found for RxCUI 1808.

alternatives.name: piretanide
alternatives.rationale: Loop diuretic, same ATC class. No FDA shortage record found for RxCUI 33770. Clinical equivalence data limited; not commonly available in US market — shortage status unverified beyond top-2 check.

alternatives.name: torsemide
alternatives.rationale: Loop diuretic, same ATC class; available as IV/PO formulations in US. Shortage status unverified (not checked per tool budget). Class-member confidence only.

citations.claim: Furosemide Injection indications (edema, acute pulmonary edema), IM/IV route, contraindications (anuria, hypersensitivity), and ototoxicity warnings
Bumetanide indications for edema (CHF, hepatic, renal); IM/IV availability; 1:40 dose conversion from furosemide; lack of cross-sensitivity with furosemide noted

items.recommended_action: Monitor inventory levels for Furosemide Injection; given zero active orders, no immediate patient impact — alert prescribers that bumetanide IM/IV (non-formulary) is an available loop-diuretic alternative if IM furosemide is needed and supply tightens.

items.summary: Furosemide Injection (IM, non-preferred) remains in active shortage as of 04/23/2026; zero active orders recorded in the past 30 days, limiting immediate patient impact.
items.rationale: today_status=Current and yesterday_status=Current with formulary_status=non-preferred and active_orders_30d=0. Rule W2 is the closest match (Current, non-preferred), but active_orders_30d=0 means there is no demonstrable current patient impact, moderating urgency. Rule W3 also applies (ongoing shortage, no status change). No rule escalates to Critical because active_orders_30d=0 does not satisfy active-orders thresholds in C1–C4. Diff bucket is listed as 'escalated,' but yesterday_status matches today_status (both Current), so no re-occurrence trigger (C3) applies; per conflict rules the more conservative classification (Watch over Resolved) is retained. Confidence ceiling applied: active_orders_30d=0 is known (not missing), but all alternatives are class-member only → max medium.

alternatives.name: bumetanide
alternatives.rationale: Same loop diuretic class (inhibits Na-K-2Cl cotransporter); labeled for IM and IV administration for edema indications overlapping furosemide. FDA label states bumetanide can be substituted at ~1:40 ratio in furosemide-allergic patients. No shortage record found for RxCUI 1808.

alternatives.name: piretanide
alternatives.rationale: Loop diuretic class member; no shortage record found for RxCUI 33770. Clinical equivalence data limited; not commonly stocked in US formularies. Shortage status unverified beyond top-2 check.

alternatives.name: torsemide
alternatives.rationale: Loop diuretic class member with established IV/oral formulations for edema. Shortage status unverified (ranked 3+, not checked per protocol).

citations.claim: Furosemide Injection shortage status is Current as of 04/23/2026; RxCUI list includes 1719290.
Furosemide Injection indicated for edema (heart failure, cirrhosis, renal disease) and acute pulmonary edema; IM and IV routes supported; contraindicated in anuria.
Bumetanide labeled for IM/IV administration; 1:40 substitution ratio for furosemide; same edema indications; cross-sensitivity with furosemide rarely observed.

items.recommended_action: No immediate patient-care action required given zero active orders; monitor formulary stock levels and confirm bumetanide IM/IV availability for contingency use if orders increase.
